title: Are the Rebuild series of films worth watching Should I watch it
display_title: Are the Rebuild series of films worth watching Should I watch it
wikitext:
[]: Are the Rebuild series of films worth watching? Should I watch it?             Quote Post by 天使 | Nyo | 天使 » Wed Sep 18, 2019 1:11 pm So, recently I've finished up watching the original Neon Genesis Evangelion series in both Sub and Dub (the ADV 1997 one, not the Netflix one) formats. But then I heard about "Rebuild of Evangelion" movies, I've heard some good and some bad about them online. But I'm legitimately curious about what YOU GUYS here think. I'm not going to watch them as of yet until the final movie is done and released, that way I can go through and watch all of them without worrying about waiting. NO SPOILERS PLEASE! My questions regarding the series of films: 1. Are they as good, better, or worse than the original series? 2. Are the characters just as well done in the movies as they are in the show? 3. Are Shinji & Kaworu still...you know, involved with each other? (I really thought that was something that was worth expanding upon since we only really got one good episode to get us introduced to Kaworu. I know he's a little more prevalent in the manga, which I'll begin reading soon to hold me over until the release of the final movie.) 4. Are there different actors for both the Sub and Dubs? (I loved the voices for the original sub and dub, and just hope that the actors/actresses are either unaltered or at least got decent replacements.) 5. How much different is the plot compared to the original show? If you got some answers to these, just let me know! I'll be sure to take a look at them ASAP. Thanks!

[]: Re: Are the Rebuild series of films worth watching? Should I watch it?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 18, 2019 1:42 pm 天使 | Nyo | 天使 wrote: But I'm legitimately curious about what YOU GUYS here think.

[]: Re: Are the Rebuild series of films worth watching? Should I watch it?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 18, 2019 2:31 pm > 1. Are they as good, better, or worse than the original series? It depends on your criteria. The movies aren't being done on a shoestring budget (time or money) as the original TV series, and have the benefit of a decade-plus of computer animation technology advances. > 2. Are the characters just as well done in the movies as they are in the show? The movies have less time available than a 26 episode TV series does. > 3. Are Shinji & Kaworu still...you know, involved with each other? Playing piano duets together, in fact. > 4. Are there different actors for both the Sub and Dubs? In each case, most of the casts reprise their roles. There are new characters in the mix, though, with new voices. > 5. How much different is the plot compared to the original show? Sort-of the same for part 1, on a diverging track in part 2, and way off into the blue yonder in part 3 and beyond

[]: Re: Are the Rebuild series of films worth watching? Should I watch it?             Quote Post by 天使 | Nyo | 天使 » Fri Sep 20, 2019 9:54 am Mr. Tines wrote: > 1. Are they as good, better, or worse than the original series? It depends on your criteria. The movies aren't being done on a shoestring budget (time or money) as the original TV series, and have the benefit of a decade-plus of computer animation technology advances. > 2. Are the characters just as well done in the movies as they are in the show? The movies have less time available than a 26 episode TV series does. > 3. Are Shinji & Kaworu still...you know, involved with each other? Playing piano duets together, in fact. > 4. Are there different actors for both the Sub and Dubs? In each case, most of the casts reprise their roles. There are new characters in the mix, though, with new voices. > 5. How much different is the plot compared to the original show? Sort-of the same for part 1, on a diverging track in part 2, and way off into the blue yonder in part 3 and beyond
